Article Title: EC announces bypolls for 3 assembly seats in Bihar, MP and Gujarat; voting on July 30
Article Text:
EC announces bypolls for 3 assembly seats in Bihar, MP and Gujarat; voting on July 30

NEW DELHI: The Election Commission on Thursday announced byelections to three assembly constituencies in Bihar, Madhya Pradesh, and Gujarat, with polling scheduled for July 30 and counting of votes on August 3.Of the three seats, two — Bankipur in Bihar and Manjalpur in Gujarat — were held by the BJP, while Datia in Madhya Pradesh was represented by the Congress.The Bankipur seat fell vacant after BJP leader Nitin Nabin resigned as an MLA following his election to the Rajya Sabha in April. Nabin was appointed the party's national president in January.The Manjalpur constituency fell vacant following the death of BJP MLA Yogeshbhai Narandas Patel in June.The Datia seat was vacated after Congress MLA Rajendra Bharti was disqualified following his conviction in a bank scam case.The Election Commission said the notification for the bypolls will be issued on July 6. The last date for filing nominations is July 13, scrutiny of nomination papers will be held on July 14, and candidates can withdraw their nominations until July 16.
Article Summary: EC announces bypolls for 3 assembly seats in Bihar, MP and Gujarat; voting on July 30. Of the three seats, two — Bankipur in Bihar and Manjalpur in Gujarat — were held by the BJP. Datia in Madhya Pradesh was represented by the Congress.

Article Title: 'Criminal trespassers': TMC vows court battle as rival faction captures party HQ
Article Text:
TMC draws battle lines after rival faction seizes party office (Image/PTI)

Poll Vote & Share your view How do you view the allegations of BJP involvement in the faction actions? As credible evidence As politically motivated 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) on Friday accused the rival faction led by expelled MLA Ritabrata Banerjee of illegally taking control of the party's organisational headquarters in Kolkata, calling its members 'criminal trespassers' and warning of legal action.The party also said it had lodged a police complaint against the rebel faction for allegedly trespassing into the office and putting locks on the premises.Speaking to reporters, TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee said, "They are criminal trespassers. They started the game and we will finish it. We will go to court."TMC leader Kunal Ghosh also criticised the police response, alleging that expelled party members had no right to enter the office."We have lodged a formal police complaint against those who trespassed into Trinamool Bhavan today and put a lock on it. There was absolutely no need to put a lock on the premises. Expelled members have no right to enter the party office," Ghosh said.He claimed that despite requests, the police allowed the rebel faction to lock the premises and later protected it."We could have easily broken the lock they installed, but we refrained from any illegal or irresponsible actions.The local police, RAF and central forces simply stood there. This is a major conspiracy to damage the Trinamool Congress. The people of Bengal and Trinamool workers are watching everything," he added.Reacting to it, TMC leader Madan Mitra accused the Central forces of protecting the rebel camp and alleged that the faction was acting on behalf of the BJP."Central Forces arrived within 15 minutes to protect a specific Trinamool Congress group. This proves that these people are acting as the BJP's 'B-team,' operating from the BJP's lap. We have not violated any laws. At least 1 lakh of our party offices in Bengal are currently shut down, and our party members cannot even sit there; they are facing oppression, hooliganism, and police attacks. No one from the BJP went to protect them," Mitra said.He also alleged that the police stopped TMC leaders from entering the premises despite having access to an adjoining room."They (Police) stated that they would summon the owner and examine the agreement held by the owner. We possess the key to the adjacent room, yet we were not allowed to enter," he added.The remarks came after the Ritabrata Banerjee-led faction took control of the party's organisational headquarters in Kolkata, a day after approaching the Election Commission to stake its claim over the Trinamool Congress name and election symbol.Ritabrata Banerjee, accompanied by senior leaders including Firhad Hakim, Javed Khan, Sandipan Saha and Akhruzzaman, held a meeting at the office and claimed that his faction represented the 'real' Trinamool Congress.Leaders aligned with the faction said they had completed the required formalities with the owners of the premises and that the office would now function as their organisational headquarters.The Metropolitan office has served as the Trinamool Congress' operational headquarters since 2022, after the party shifted there while its original office was undergoing reconstruction.The dispute between the two factions intensified after the party's defeat in the recent West Bengal Assembly elections, with both camps claiming to be the legitimate Trinamool Congress.On Thursday, a delegation led by Ritabrata Banerjee met the full bench of the Election Commission in New Delhi to press its claim over the party's organisational leadership.The Election Commission has asked both factions to submit their respective claims and supporting documents on the party's organisational structure, authorised signatories and control of the party by 5.30 pm on July 6.
Article Summary: TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee: "They are criminal trespassers. They started the game and we will finish it. We will go to court." TMC leader Kunal Ghosh also criticised the police response, alleging that expelled party members had no right to enter the office.

Article Title: Congress demands dissolution of Ram temple trust
Article Text:
Jairam Ramesh

NEW DELHI: Congress Friday demanded dissolution of the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust over the donation theft row and sought a SC-monitored probe. It demanded that PM Narendra Modi break his silence on the issue."Mr PM, when there has been a betrayal of Lord Ram and public's trust has been breached, what hesitation or arrogance is there in accepting it?" Congress' Jairam Ramesh asked on X, adding, "There is anger and outrage across country against the theft of donations that took place under the leadership of people from the RSS ecosystem chosen by your govt. What you have done was a grave sin indeed, but your silence on this extremely important matter now is a bigger sin. "The party added that "clean chit" given by RSS members in the matter was "disgraceful and shameful". TNN
Article Summary: Congress demands dissolution of Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust over donation theft row. Party demands a SC-monitored probe and demands that PM Narendra Modi break his silence on the issue. Party added that "clean chit" given by RSS members in the matter was "disgraceful and shameful"

Article Title: Govt fixing polls with EC's help: INDIA bloc to CJI
Article Text:
Govt fixing polls with EC's help: INDIA bloc to CJI

Who next if courts too fail? INDIA bloc to CJI

NEW DELHI: Claiming that democracy is in "jeopardy" because the BJP govt is "manipulating" elections with the help of a "biased" Election Commission, the joint opposition has urged CJI Surya Kant to suspend SIR of electoral rolls, arguing it should be done five years before an assembly election.The opposition also raised questions about EVMs and sought serious consideration to restore ballot papers, while alleging that polls in Delhi, Maharashtra and Haryana were manipulated. INDIA bloc, in its June 28 letter to CJI, stated it would not have written to him but has been forced by circumstances. In a list of its grievances - SIR to "favour BJP", "misuse of agencies to manipulate polls", and appointment of ECs - it hinted it has hit a wall with govt.The SIR process was upheld by Supreme Court in May as it observed that the exercise advances the constitutional imperative of free and fair elections."Democracies turn into anarchies when institutional mechanisms fail completely... We are not questioning judiciary. In fact, we turn to courts when every mechanism fails. When this, too, fails, it leaves open the question - who do we now turn to? We leave that question for you to ponder upon," the bloc of 24 political parties and an Independent MP wrote to CJI."Judges do not live in ivory towers. You, too, are aware of what is happening on the ground," it said.The letter alleged that under BJP, poll manipulation starts with the appointment of election commissioners, as Modi govt removed CJI from the collegium to select ECs, contrary to Supreme Court's judgment in Anoop Baranwal case. It alleged govt has appointed people who are "closely associated with it"."The reason for our grave concern is the brazen biased conduct of the EC, in particular chief election commissioner," it said. "There has been open, unabashed support of the BJP during the course of electoral processes," the bloc said, calling SIR the "unkindest cut" by EC and CEC Gyanesh Kumar.
Article Summary: Joint opposition has urged CJI Surya Kant to suspend SIR of electoral rolls. Opposition also raised questions about EVMs and sought serious consideration to restore ballot papers. Opposition alleges that polls in Delhi, Maharashtra and Haryana were manipulated.

Article Title: ESAKKI SUBAYA CALLS ON SPEAKER J C D PRABHAKAR TO SUBMIT EXPLANATION FOR HIS RESIGNATION
Article Text:
Esakki Subaya

Chennai:





Former Ambasamudram MLA Esakki Subaya called on Speaker J C D Prabhakar at the secretariat on Friday and submitted his explanation for resigning from the MLA post.The explanation was in response to the show-cause notice issued to him following a representation from AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami . EPS has sought action against the party MLAs for `cross-voting’ during the motion of confidence in the assembly.Three other MLAs who resigned their post along with Esakki Subaya had appeared before the Speaker and submitted their explanations few days back.AIADMK has approached the Madras high court against the Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker for accepting the resignations of the rebel MLAs despite proceedings under the anti-defection law being pending.
Article Summary: Former Ambasamudram MLA Esakki Subaya called on Speaker J C D Prabhakar at the secretariat on Friday and submitted his explanation for resigning from the MLA post. The explanation was in response to the show-cause notice issued to him following a representation from AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami.

Article Text:
The contrabands were destroyed in the presence of city police commissioner N Kannan, and deputy commissioners of police G Karthikeyan and P Thangadurai.





COIMBATORE: More than 270 kg of drugs seized from peddlers in Coimbatore city were incinerated at the Kovai Bio-waste Management Facility at Orrattukuppai on Thursday.These contrabands, seized in 140 Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) cases, were incinerated after respective courts directed police to destroy them at a bio-waste treatment facility. They included 270 kg of ganja, 334 gram of methamphetamine, 1.2 kg of ganja chocolates, 39 mg of hashish and 5,168 painkiller tablets.The contrabands were destroyed in the presence of city police commissioner N Kannan, and deputy commissioners of police G Karthikeyan and P Thangadurai.
Article Summary: More than 270 kg of drugs seized from peddlers in Coimbatore city were incinerated at the Kovai Bio-waste Management Facility at Orrattukuppai on Thursday. The contrabands were seized in 140 Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) cases.

Article Title: Guest worker arrested for sexually assaulting minor girl in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Coimbatore:





A 50-year-old guest worker from Bihar was arrested on charges of sexually assaulting a 10-year-old girl with developmental disorders near Thudiyalur in the city on Wednesday.Police said the accused, is a relative of the survivor and resides near her house. The incident took place when the girl’s parents were not at home. The girl eventually informed her mother about her ordeal, following which a complaint was lodged at the All Women Police (Central) station.The accused was booked under Section 5(m) (penetrative sexual assault on child below 12 years) and 6 (punishment of aggravated penetrative sexual assault) of the Protection of Children from Sexual Assault (Pocso) Act. He was produced before the court and remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: Police said the accused, is a relative of the survivor and resides near her house. The incident took place when the girl’s parents were not at home. The girl eventually informed her mother about her ordeal, following which a complaint was lodged at the All Women Police (Central) station.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: DYFI activist gets bail in Kafir screenshot case
Article Text:
Jithin Bhaskar

Kozhikode

:





Kozhikode principal sessions court on Friday granted bail to DYFI activist Jithin Bhaskar, who was arrested by special investigation team ( SIT ) in the ‘Kafir screenshot’ case that triggered a major political row during the 2024 Lok Sabha election campaign in Vadakara.Bhaskar, a member of DYFI Vadakara block committee and CPM’s Thuruthi local committee, was arrested by SIT on June 16. He had approached the sessions court after Vadakara JFCM court rejected his bail plea twice.SIT had found that Bhaskar was the first one to forward the screenshot in the WhatsApp group named ‘Vadakara Squad’ and that he also had role in fabricating the fake screenshot. It also found that he had personally forwarded the screenshot to around 200 persons.During the bail hearing, Bhaskar told the court that the case against him was politically motivated and that he had been cooperating with the investigation and that he has completed over two weeks in judicial custody and police had interrogated in custody as well.SIT had booked him under various IPC provisions, including charges related to promoting communal disharmony, attempting to incite unrest and destruction of evidence.The screenshot reportedly portrayed LDF candidate Shailaja as a ‘kafir’ and UDF candidate Shafi Parambil as a pious person, sparking controversy during the poll campaign. The ‘fabricated screenshot’ was circulated in the name of MYL activist Mohammed Khasim.SIT had found that Khasim had no role in creating the screenshot and had filed a report to the court seeking dropping him from the accused list.
Article Summary: Jithin Bhaskar was arrested by special investigation team ( SIT ) in the ‘Kafir screenshot’ case that triggered a major political row during the 2024 Lok Sabha election campaign in Vadakara. SIT had booked him under various IPC provisions, including charges related to promoting communal disharmony and attempting to incite unrest.

Article Title: Kerala HC stays proceedings in case against businessman Bobby Chemmanur for sexually coloured remarks
Article Text:
Bobby Chemmanur

Kochi

:





High court on Friday stayed the further proceedings in a case against businessman Bobby Chemmanur, who is accused of making sexually coloured remarks against a Malayalam film actor at the inaugural function of one of his business establishments and subsequently subjecting her to cyber harassment.Justice Jobin Sebastian issued the order while considering a petition by Chemmanur seeking to quash the proceedings in the case, pending before the additional sessions court, Ernakulam, which had been registered on a complaint lodged by the actor. The interim stay order for three weeks came after HC considered an affidavit filed by the actor stating that the dispute between her and Chemmanur had been amicably settled.Earlier, Chemmanur had tendered a public apology through his Instagram page, stating that he had realised his remarks had caused distress to the actor and her family. He also apologised for the harm caused by his remarks and subsequent actions. The actor later accepted the apology and filed an affidavit supporting Chemmanur’s plea, stating that the dispute had been settled. Taking note of the affidavit and the submissions, HC stayed the further proceedings in the case and adjourned the petition for a detailed hearing.The case arose from an incident during the inaugural function of Chemmanur’s jewellery store at Alacode in Kannur in Aug 2024, where he allegedly made sexually coloured remarks against the actor, who was also present at the event. Acting on her complaint, the Ernakulam Central Police arrested Chemmanur on Jan 8, 2025. He was later released on bail.
Article Summary: Bobby Chemmanur is accused of making sexually coloured remarks against a Malayalam film actor at the inaugural function of one of his business establishments. He is also accused of subjecting her to cyber harassment. The actor has filed an affidavit stating that the dispute between her and Chem manur had been amicably settled.
